steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage first describes how genes pass phenotypes (earlobe shape, hair texture) from parents to children today. The final sentence shifts to a different time frame: the child’s own descendants. The transition must signal that shift to the future — not an example, clarification, or contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: introduces an illustration of the preceding point. The final sentence is not an example of how genes work today; it projects forward in time. ✗
  • Choice B — Someday,: signals a future time, matching the shift from present inheritance to what will happen when the child eventually has descendants. ✓
  • Choice C — To be exact,: signals a clarification or narrowing of the previous statement. The final sentence broadens the scope to future generations, not a refinement. ✗
  • Choice D — Instead,: signals a contrast or replacement of the preceding idea. The final sentence does not contradict or replace what was said about parental inheritance. ✗
That is the correct answer.
